elk: noun. Any of various large species of deer such as the red deer, moose or wapiti (see usage notes).
koel: noun. A cuckoo of the genus Eudynamys, native to Asia, Australia and the Pacific.
kolo: noun. A national folk dance common in regions pertaining to South Slavic people, performed in a circle.
kor: noun. Alternative form of cor: a former Hebrew and Phoenician unit of volume.
kore: noun. An Ancient Greek statue of a woman, portrayed standing, usually clothed, painted in bright colours and having an elaborate hairstyle.
lek: noun. An aggregation of male animals for the purposes of courtship and display.
lev: noun. The currency of Bulgaria from 1880 to 2025; divided into 100 stotinki.
loo: noun. A lavatory: a room used for urination and defecation.
look: verb. To try to see, to pay attention to with one’s eyes.
looker: noun. One that looks or is actively looking; a watcher; an observer.
lore: noun. All the facts and traditions about a particular subject that have been accumulated over time through education or experience.
love: noun. A deep caring for the existence of another.
lover: noun. One who loves and cares for another person in a romantic way; a sweetheart, love, soulmate, boyfriend, girlfriend, or spouse.
ole: interjection. An interjection used to stir up excitement.
oleo: noun. A type of energy -absorbing landing gear strut in which sudden compression or extension of the strut causes a gas -filled chamber to
ore: noun. Rock or other material that contains valuable or utilitarian materials; primarily a rock containing metals or gems for which it is
orle: noun. A bordure that runs around the outline of a shield without touching the edge.
overlook: noun. A vista or point that gives a view down toward something else.
ovolo: noun. A classical convex moulding carved with an egg and dart ornament.
relook: noun. An additional look or examination.
rev: verb. To increase the speed of a motor, or to operate at a higher speed.
role: noun. A character or part played by a performer or actor.
rook: noun. A European bird, Corvus frugilegus, of the crow family.
voe: noun. A narrow sea inlet, particularly in the Shetland Islands, similar to a fjord.
vole: noun. Any of a large number of species of small rodents of the tribes Arvicolini, Ellobiusini, Clethrionomyini, Pliomyini, Phenacomyini and
eorl: noun. An Anglo-Saxon of noble rank; a nobleman ranking above a thane; alderman.
erk: noun. A member of the groundcrew in the RAF.
kev: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Kanikkaran.
koe: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Kacipo-Balesi.
kroo: noun. An obsolete African unit of measure, varying by the type of produce.
loke: noun. The wicket or hatch of a door.
lor: particle. Tagged at the end of a sentence to convey a sense of resignation or inevitability.
oker: noun. Interest on money; usury; increase.
oor: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Oorlams.
oreo: noun. Alternative letter-case form of Oreo.
orlo: noun. A Spanish wind instrument, possibly a crumhorn.
roke: noun. Fog, mist; light rain; smoke, vapour; damp.
